questions.question: The Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training is delivered at two tiers. Which tier applies to most care workers who provide care and support directly?
options: A separate tier only for family carers
No tier is needed for care workers
Tier 1
Tier 2

questions.question: Which of the following is a defining feature of the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training?
options: It can be completed instead of speaking to your manager about your tier
It is co-delivered together with people who have lived experience of learning disability and autism
It is optional for care workers who already feel confident
It is delivered only as a written test

questions.question: The duty to make reasonable adjustments is described as 'anticipatory'. What does this mean in practice?
options: Services should plan ahead rather than wait for a problem to arise
Only large organisations are required to make them
Adjustments can only be made after a complaint is received
Adjustments are optional if they would cost any money

questions.question: Under which law must your employer ensure you complete the Oliver McGowan Mandatory Training at the tier appropriate to your role?
options: The Equality Act 2010
The Mental Capacity Act 2005
The Care Standards Act
The Health and Care Act 2022

questions.question: What is the purpose of a hospital passport (also called a health passport)?
options: To act as legal proof of a person's diagnosis
To permanently replace the person's GP records
To give staff who do not know the person the essential information they need to provide safe, personalised care quickly
To record staff working hours on a ward

questions.question: According to the course, what can the effort of masking lead to for an autistic person?
options: A permanent reduction in the support they need
Improved long-term physical health
A cure for their autism
Burnout, anxiety, or a delayed 'crash' once the person is somewhere they feel safe

questions.question: Before anyone concludes that a person cannot make a particular decision, what must happen first?
options: The decision must be delayed for a set period of 28 days
The decision must be made for them by a manager
They must prove their capacity in writing
They must be given all practicable support to make the decision themselves

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a shutdown?
options: An inward response where the person goes quiet or withdrawn, which can be easy to miss
A deliberate attempt to gain attention
A loud, outward response with shouting and sudden movement
A sign that the person is no longer overwhelmed

questions.question: Which statement best reflects the 'least restrictive principle'?
options: Restriction should be used routinely so that people stay safe
Restrictions do not need to be recorded or reviewed
The most secure option is always the best option
Any restriction must be the minimum necessary, used only to prevent harm, for the shortest time, and in the person's best interests

questions.question: People with a learning disability aged 14 and over are entitled to a free annual health check from their GP, even if they feel well.
options: True
False

questions.question: Making a reasonable adjustment means giving a disabled person an unfair advantage over everyone else.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of these are examples of reasonable adjustments in practice? (Select all that apply)
options: Asking the person to re-explain their needs at every single visit
Allowing longer appointments so that nothing is rushed
Increasing psychotropic medication to keep the person calm
Providing a quieter room with dimmed lights
Offering the first or last appointment of the day

questions.question: You are supporting someone under the STOMP approach. Which of these are part of your role? (Select all that apply)
options: Helping make sure medicines are reviewed and questioned, not just repeated
Supporting alternatives such as good communication, routine, and activity to reduce distress
Changing or stopping the person's medication yourself
Observing and recording the effects and side effects of medicines
Aiming to stop all of the person's medicines as quickly as possible

questions.question: When some of a person's senses are heightened, so that everyday noise can feel painful or exhausting, they are described as ___.
STOMP works alongside ___, which promotes the same safe approach to medicines for children and young people.
